# RedwoodSDK: Type-Safe Navigation with linkFor

## Background

A RedwoodSDK project is pre-installed at `/home/user/myapp`. Use `linkFor` from `rwsdk/router` to build a typed `link` helper, and use it inside server components so that every link in your HTML output is generated from the typed helper (no hard-coded path strings on the page).

## Requirements

Extend `src/worker.tsx` with the routes below and create a `src/app/shared/links.ts` file that exports a typed `link` constant created from `linkFor<App>()` (where `App` is the type of the default export from `src/worker.tsx`). Use that `link` helper inside the page components.

## Acceptance Criteria

* Project path: /home/user/myapp
* Start command: npm run dev -- --host 0.0.0.0 --port 5173
* Port: 5173
* Required file: `/home/user/myapp/src/app/shared/links.ts` must exist and export a value named `link`.
* Routes to implement (all under the existing `render(Document, ...)` block):
  * `GET /home`: server component HTML must contain an `<a>` element whose `href` is generated by calling `link("/about")` and whose visible text is `About`.
  * `GET /home`: server component HTML must also contain an `<a>` whose `href` is generated by calling `link("/users/:id", { id: "42" })` and whose visible text is `Show user 42`.
  * `GET /about`: HTML must contain an `<a>` whose `href` is generated by calling `link("/home")` and whose visible text is `Back to home`.
  * `GET /users/:id`: HTML must contain the text `User profile: <id>` (with the URL param substituted).
Let me start by exploring the project structure to understand what's already there.

output.content:
export const Document: React.FC<{ children: React.ReactNode }> = ({
  children,
}) => (
  <html lang="en">
    <head>
      <meta charSet="utf-8" />
      <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
      <title>@redwoodjs/starter-minimal</title>
      <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com" />
      <link
        rel="preconnect"
        href="https://fonts.gstatic.com"
        crossOrigin=""
      />
      <link
        rel="stylesheet"
        href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Noto+Sans:ital,wght@0,100..900;1,100..900&family=Playfair+Display:ital,wght@0,400..900;1,400..900&display=optional"
        precedence="first"
      />
      <link rel="modulepreload" href="/src/client.tsx" />
    </head>
    <body>
      {children}
      <script>import("/src/client.tsx")</script>
    </body>
  </html>
);

However, looking at the current `links.ts`, it imports `App` from `rwsdk/worker` but the actual app type should be the default export from `src/worker.tsx`. Let me check how this needs to work:

todos.content: Fix src/app/shared/links.ts to use correct App type from worker.tsx
Add /home, /about, /users/:id routes to src/worker.tsx
Create About and UserProfile page components using link helper
Update Home page component to use link helper with <a> elements

part.text:
The current `links.ts` imports `App` from `rwsdk/worker` but it should import the default export type from `src/worker.tsx`. Let me look at this more carefully:

The `linkFor<App>()` expects `App` to have `__rwRoutes` on it (from the type definition). The default export of `src/worker.tsx` is `defineApp(...)` which returns an `AppDefinition<Routes, T>` that has `__rwRoutes`. So `App` in `links.ts` should be the type of the default export from `src/worker.tsx`.

Let me now implement everything:

part.text: The `/home` route is working correctly. Let me verify the other routes:

input.command: curl -s http://localhost:5173/about 2>&1 | grep -o ']*>[^<]*' && echo "---" && curl -s http://localhost:5173/users/42 2>&1 | grep -o 'User profile[^<]*'

part.text: The `/about` route shows the link correctly. But `/users/42` shows the id as empty. Let me check how params are accessed in route handlers:

The rendered HTML contains `User profile: <!-- -->42` (the React rendered output with a comment node between the text and the param). The actual HTML is correct — `params.id` is being rendered as `42`. The `<!-- -->` is just React's comment placeholder for expressions. Let me verify the full rendered HTML for `/users/42`:
All routes are working correctly. Let me do a final check on all three routes to confirm everything is as expected:

[^<]+

'

part.text: All routes are working perfectly. Let me verify the `links.ts` file exists and exports `link`:

todos.content: Add /home, /about, /users/:id routes to src/worker.tsx

Update Home page component to use link helper with <a> elements

input.result: All requirements are implemented and verified. Here's a summary of what was done: **`src/app/shared/links.ts`** — exports a typed `link` constant using `linkFor()` where `app` is the default export from `src/worker.tsx`: ```ts import { linkFor } from "rwsdk/router"; import type app from "@/worker"; export const link = linkFor(); ``` **`src/worker.tsx`** — extended with three new routes under the `render(Document, [...])` block: - `GET /home` → `Home` component - `GET /about` → `About` component - `GET /users/:id` → `UserProfile` component **`src/app/pages/home.tsx`** — uses `link("/about")` and `link("/users/:id", { id: "42" })` to produce: - `About` - `Show user 42` **`src/app/pages/about.tsx`** — uses `link("/home")` to produce: - `Back to home` **`src/app/pages/userProfile.tsx`** — renders `User profile: {params.id}` from the URL parameter. Live verification: - `GET /home` → contains `About` and `Show user 42` ✅ - `GET /about` → contains `Back to home` ✅ - `GET /users/42` → contains `User profile: 42` ✅
